items.summary: Ecologists say thousands of hours of audio recordings have detected night parrot calls in south-west Queensland.

items.title: The sound of a night parrot recorded at a new location in southwest Queensland

items.title: Australian golf junior hoping to test his skills at Canadian invitational
items.summary: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

items.title: About 100 GPs now qualified to diagnose ADHD in SA with calls to expand to regions
items.summary: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

items.title: Iran daily briefing: Fragile ceasefire extended, along with anxiety and confusion
items.summary: The Iran war ceasefire has been extended but no-one is quite sure on the new deadline, Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran writes.

items.title: 'Terrifying' experience for Bunbury family as wild storm rips roof off their home
items.summary: A woman describes her "terrifying" ordeal after a powerful cold front which sounded like a "jet plane" tore the roof off her family home while she was inside it on Monday night.

items.title: Meme warfare and 'slopaganda' are defining the AI-era Iran war
items.summary: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

items.title: Remote NT residents evacuated to Batchelor say security 'treating us like prisoners'
items.summary: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

items.title: Leicester City relegated to League One 10 years after Premier League title
items.summary: Ten years after a fairytale Premier League title and five years since winning the FA Cup, Leicester City is destined for the third tier of English football.

items.title: US authorities probe possible ChatGPT involvement in university shooting
items.summary: US authorities are investigating if there was any criminal behaviour involving interactions between Open AI's ChatGPT app and a man accused of killing two people and injuring six others in a shooting at Florida State University.

items.title: Southern NSW family's push for affordable eczema medication so kids can have a normal childhood
items.summary: The cost of relief for kids under 12 with severe eczema is $1,600 a month, but a push for PBS funding is looking to change that.

items.title: Fire conditions on a 'knife edge' in Queensland's south as burn window tightens
items.summary: A Queensland Rural Fire Service superintendent says fire conditions in parts of the state are on the edge after receiving very little rain this wet season.

items.title: Queensland Maroons name Tarryn Aiken, Ali Brigginshaw replacements for Women's State of Origin
items.summary: With stalwart halves Ali Brigginshaw and Tarryn Aiken unavailable for selection, the Maroons name a new-look Queensland side for the first Women's State of Origin game in Newcastle.

items.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace his Anzac relative's World War I footsteps
items.summary: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

items.title: Kyle Flanagan benched as Dean Young names first Dragons NRL team, fires assistant coach Michael Ennis
items.summary: Dean Young names the first team of his tenure as Dragons coach, dropping Kyle Flanagan to the bench and sacking assistant and attack coach Michael Ennis.

items.title: Papunya residents living in extreme heat sue NT government over unsafe housing
items.summary: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

items.title: Australian-first lawsuit over 'extreme heat' risks in remote NT public housing
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: What 100 years of Australian soil samples in our national archive can tell us about our history and future
items.summary: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

items.title: Donald Trump extends Iran ceasefire but continues Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he will hold off attacking Iran until Tehran's negotiating teams come up with a unified proposal for a peace deal.

items.title: A rare glimpse inside the services helping NSW women leaving custody get clean
items.summary: Guthrie House is one of the few residential rehabilitation services in NSW designed to help women with current involvement with the criminal justice system get back on track.

items.title: FOI documents reveal Adelaide's COP31 considerations included gift bags and 'flotels'
items.summary: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

items.title: Refurbished facilities and funding for new units locked in for Hobart Women's Shelter
items.summary: A women's shelter struggling to meet enormous demand says it will soon be able to almost double its capacity.

items.title: Junior clubs, former player and neuroscientists welcome new NRL guidelines to limit head knocks but some say it's not enough
items.summary: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

items.title: Carpooling helps to slash fuel prices, so why aren't more people doing it?
items.summary: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

items.title: Closure of AEIOU centres for children with autism after NDIS cuts leaves lives upended
items.summary: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

items.title: Victorians transitioning from gas exacerbates growing problem of undervoltage
items.summary: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

items.title: Butler urged to end 'limbo' for 500,000 Australians with psychosocial disability in NDIS reforms
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

items.title: Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art sees how 24 artists working across the country Yield Strength
items.summary: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

items.title: Whether it's grief or liberation, family estrangement affects many of us
items.summary: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 22: We're off for a trip around the world
items.summary: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

items.title: Several ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran calls on US to lift blockade — as it happened
items.summary: Three ships have come under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, maritime and security sources say, after Iran said it would not participate in peace talks until the US lifts its blockade. Look back on how it unfolded.

items.title: Government backs down on charging older Australians $50/hour for showers
items.summary: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

items.title: First responders unable to help mother bleeding in Darwin shelter for almost an hour, inquest hears
items.summary: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Captain stranded in Strait of Hormuz tells of being trapped amid Iran war
items.summary: For months, seafarers have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. Tanker captain Raman Kapoor is speaking out about the fears and uncertainty of his crew.

items.title: Queensland Tourism Industry Council calls on government for support as tourists cancel bookings
items.summary: A Far North Queensland caravan park manager says more people are ringing up to cancel than to book a spot with concerns over fuel prices and availability.

items.title: Former civil servant says there was political pressure to approve Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to US
items.summary: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

items.title: NSW council investigates Kempsey Shire councillor over online comments
items.summary: A Kempsey Shire councillor is being investigated for misconduct after allegedly claiming a woman should have been "bashed" online.

items.title: Expectant parents and ED patients diverted as two regional hospitals struggle with workforce shortages
items.summary: Two regional hospitals, 30 minutes' drive apart, struggle to consistently provide routine medical services, forcing repeated patient diversions.

items.title: Vanuatu denies it is about to sign a security deal with China as Australia works to ink its own pact
items.summary: As Australia presses on to finally sign a key agreement with Vanuatu, reports of a security agreement with China have drawn a fierce response from Port Vila.

items.title: Tom Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Elijah Hollands episode
items.summary: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

items.title: Wollongong researchers working to create shark-deterrent surfboard fins
items.summary: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

items.title: States and territories left in the dark on NDIS savings
items.summary: State treasurers say a meeting on NDIS reforms to be announced tomorrow was light on details, leaving states and territories waiting with everyone else to find out how a new round of savings measures will affect the disability scheme — and their budgets.

items.title: Charges for CSL Australia over environmental impacts from Goliath crash with tugboats
items.summary: The owner of a bulk cement carrier has been charged with environmental offences following a 2022 crash that sank two tugboats in north-west Tasmania.

items.title: China opens the gate to more Aussie beef with new export licences for several facilities
items.summary: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

items.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after state election
items.summary: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

items.title: ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury denies quitting over Liberals coalition talks, but they can't have helped
items.summary: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

items.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump's cabinet after abuse of power allegations
items.summary: The Labor secretary's departure was announced by a White House aide on Monday, local time.

items.title: The Drop music festival still owes ticketholders refunds, six years after cancelled shows
items.summary: Ticketholders are still waiting for refunds and answers from The Drop music festival.

items.title: Israel's occupation of south Lebanon spells 'bleak' future despite ceasefire, experts say
items.summary: Experts say Israel's continued presence in south Lebanon is creating a "bleak" future as it destroys villages in what it is calling a "buffer zone" and a "Forward Defense Line".

items.title: Elijah Hollands's dad posts message about Carlton player's mental health struggle
items.summary: Elijah Hollands's father, former AFL player Ben Hollands, posts on social media pledging to help lift his son up and urging others to check on their friends if they are struggling.

items.title: About 2,000 fines withdrawn since AI road safety cameras introduced in WA
items.summary: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

items.title: Second death of Aboriginal inmate in Bandyup Women's Prison sparks call for review
items.summary: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

items.title: What I learnt about myself after quitting TV for a month
items.summary: It was partly an experiment in self-control and habit-breaking, but also a response to the unhappy realisation that watching shows had become my crutch to zone out.

items.title: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon survives confidence vote
items.summary: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he has the support of his party after calling and winning a vote of confidence in his leadership.

items.title: US presses Cuba for reforms on political freedoms in meeting held in Havana
items.summary: Cuba says the exchange was conducted "respectfully and professionally" and that the US delegation did not issue any threats or deadlines.

items.title: US, Philippine and Australian troops begin largest military exercise in 'real-world conditions'
items.summary: Troops from the US, Philippines, Australia and Japan unite for the largest military exercise in "real-world conditions" to ensure security in the Indo-Pacific region. But China has criticised the drill.

items.title: Australian War Memorial curator amazed to find 4 metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes
items.summary: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

items.title: Turtles 'awaiting their fate' buried under mud at NSW wetlands after inflows halted
items.summary: Researchers in north-west NSW are wading through thick mud in an attempt to save hundreds of turtles after the NSW government decided to stop supplying water to the protected wetland.

items.title: Dean Young named St George Illawarra Dragons coach after Shane Flanagan's exit
items.summary: After about 12 hours without a head coach, the Dragons appoint assistant Dean Young to lead the winless side for "at least" the rest of the 2026 NRL season.

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down and hand reins to iPhone maker's hardware leader
items.summary: Apple has announced the chief executive who took over from Steve Jobs is to step down and has named his successor.

items.title: Indonesian fisher says poverty behind illegal fishing in Australian waters
items.summary: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

items.title: Native title holders say they have been sidelined by Rex Minerals over SA copper mine
items.summary: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

items.title: Volunteers who helped find Celine Cremer plan new missing person search in Tasmanian wilderness
items.summary: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

items.title: Students thank Australian Defence Force veterans with postcards ahead of Anzac Day
items.summary: Thousands of children across Queensland are sending postcards to former and serving Australian Defence Force members.
